blogposts.title: Slow Color: Introduction to Natural Dyes on Textiles
blogposts.content: Natural dyes offer a slower, more considered approach to color on cloth. In this introduction, I cover the basics of cellulose vs. protein fibers, how to prepare fabric with simple mordants, and a few of my favorite starter plants for dyeing at home. We’ll walk through an easy first dye bath and discuss how to keep notes so you can reliably repeat your favorite hues.
blogposts.excerpt: An introductory guide to setting up your first natural dye bath for textiles, from fabric preparation to simple plant-based colors.

blogposts.title: Indigo and Iron: Deep Blues with Natural Dyes
blogposts.content: Blue is one of the most sought-after colors in natural dyeing. This article explores working with indigo vats alongside iron modifiers to build depth and shadow into your textiles. I share my current vat recipe, safe handling practices, and a step-by-step process for creating layered shibori-inspired patterns on cotton and linen.
blogposts.excerpt: Explore indigo vats, iron modifiers, and layered resist techniques for rich, complex blue textiles.

blogposts.title: Botanical Neutrals: Warm Browns from Natural Dye Baths
blogposts.content: Warm neutrals are the quiet backbone of many of my wall hangings and cushions. In this post, I focus on extracting soft browns and caramels from walnut hulls, onion skins, and cutch. You’ll learn how to shift tones with pH changes, when to add iron for muted charcoals, and how I combine these colors in the studio for a cohesive palette.
blogposts.excerpt: Learn to create a palette of warm, botanical browns using walnut, onion skins, and cutch on natural fibers.

blogposts.title: Troubleshooting Natural Dyeing on Linen and Cotton
blogposts.content: Uneven color, unexpected fading, or pale, washed-out hues are common challenges when dyeing cellulose fibers like linen and cotton. Here I break down the most frequent issues I see in student work, from skipped scouring to rushed mordanting. Each problem is paired with before-and-after photos and a checklist you can use the next time you set up a dye session.
blogposts.excerpt: A practical checklist for resolving patchy color, fading, and other common natural dye problems on cellulose fibers.

blogposts.title: Studio Notes: January 2026
blogposts.content: The start of the year is always a time for reorganizing the studio, reviewing last year’s collections, and sketching new work. In this entry I walk through my current loom setup, the yarns I’m most excited about, and how the Spring 2025 collection is informing a new series of smaller cushion covers.
blogposts.excerpt: A behind-the-scenes look at the studio in January 2026, including loom setups and upcoming textile experiments.

blogposts.title: Weaving Texture: Simple Loom Techniques for Dimension
blogposts.content: Texture is central to my textile work. This article introduces a handful of approachable techniques—including rya knots, soumak, and clasped weft—that you can use on a simple frame loom to add depth to wall hangings. I include diagrams, yarn suggestions, and finishing tips.
blogposts.excerpt: Add dimension to your woven textiles with a few beginner-friendly texture techniques.

blogposts.title: Hand Quilting Details for Textile Wall Hangings
blogposts.content: Small hand quilted details can completely change the way a wall hanging catches light. Here I share how I stabilize layered textiles, choose thread weights, and plan hand-quilted motifs that complement woven or appliquéd surfaces without overwhelming them.
blogposts.excerpt: How to add subtle hand-quilted details to wall hangings for extra structure and light play.

blogposts.title: Designing Cushion Covers for Cozy Corners
blogposts.content: Cushion covers are a gentle way to bring textile art into everyday life. In this post I consider scale, color blocking, and durability when designing cushions for reading nooks and sofas. I also share a few examples from recent commissions and how I matched them to existing interiors.
blogposts.excerpt: Thoughts on scale, color, and durability when designing textile cushion covers for the home.

blogposts.title: Behind the Scenes of the Spring 2025 Collection
blogposts.content: The Spring 2025 collection, "Tactile Horizons", grew out of sketchbook studies of coastal lines and woven grids. This article walks through my initial drawings, dye tests, and sample swatches, and shows how they became the final wall hangings and cushions in the collection.
blogposts.excerpt: Sketches, dye tests, and samples that led to the Spring 2025 "Tactile Horizons" collection.

blogposts.title: Exhibition Diary: London 2023
blogposts.content: My 2023 London exhibition brought together works from several collections in an intimate gallery setting. In this diary entry I share installation photos, notes from the opening night, and the conversations that have stayed with me since.
blogposts.excerpt: Reflections and images from the 2023 exhibition of my textile work in London.

blogposts.title: A Brief History of Natural Dyes
blogposts.content: Long before synthetic dyes, makers used plants, insects, and minerals to color cloth. This article traces a brief history of natural dyes across cultures, from madder and weld in Europe to cochineal in Central and South America. It also touches on how contemporary textile artists are revisiting these traditions.
blogposts.excerpt: An overview of how natural dyes have shaped textile history around the world.

blogposts.title: Organizing a Small Textile Studio
blogposts.content: Working in a small studio means every shelf and basket has to earn its keep. In this post I share how I store yarns, fabric remnants, and dye materials, along with a few labeling tricks that make it easier to find exactly what I need mid-project.
blogposts.excerpt: Storage strategies and labeling tips for keeping a compact textile studio functional.

blogposts.title: Working with Recycled Fibers in Textile Art
blogposts.content: Recycled fibers—from mill ends to unravelled sweaters—play a big role in my practice. Here I discuss how I source them, what to watch out for when combining different fiber contents, and how I plan compositions around the materials I already have.
blogposts.excerpt: Ideas for sourcing and working with recycled yarns and fabrics in woven and quilted pieces.

blogposts.title: Slow Craft and Mindful Making
blogposts.content: Textile work invites slowness: the steady rhythm of stitching, the repetition of warping a loom. This essay reflects on how slowing down in the studio has changed the way I think about time, productivity, and value in my practice.
blogposts.excerpt: An essay on the role of slowness and attention in textile craft.

collections.title: Spring 2025: Tactile Horizons
collections.description: A collection of medium- and large-scale textile wall hangings and cushions inspired by coastal horizons and woven grids, featuring layered natural-dyed blues and warm earth tones.

collections.title: Autumn 2024: Earthbound Layers
collections.description: Textile pieces built from stacked, quilted shapes and densely woven textures, exploring soil strata and city pavements in rusts, charcoals, and muted greens.

collections.title: Summer 2024: Indigo Drift
collections.description: Lightweight wall hangings and cushions dyed in layered indigo baths, with stitched and woven motifs that echo tidal lines and currents.

collections.title: Spring 2024: Woven Light
collections.description: A series of luminous textiles that play with transparency, open weave structures, and hand-stitched highlights to catch shifting daylight.

collections.title: Autumn 2023: Urban Textures
collections.description: Dense, tactile wall hangings drawing on brickwork, scaffolding, and weathered facades, translated into layered cloth and stitched line.

collections.title: Summer 2023: Orchard Lines
collections.description: Smaller textile pieces exploring the rhythm of tree rows and fruit-laden branches, with hand-dyed yellows, pinks, and soft greens.

collections.title: Spring 2023: Soft Grid
collections.description: An early exploration of grid-based compositions, combining pieced cloth, gentle quilting, and restrained color palettes.

collections.title: Winter 2022: Night Weave
collections.description: Dark, cocooning textiles built from deep blues, charcoals, and midnight greens, with subtle flashes of metallic thread.

workshopregistrations.notes: Looking forward to learning more about textile techniques in May 2025.
Joining from a small apartment, will be working on cotton only.
Attending with a friend, both complete beginners.
Especially interested in warm earth tones for home decor projects.
Please let me know about accessibility details for the studio space.

workshops.title: Indigo Immersion: In-Studio Vat Basics
workshops.description: A one-day in-studio workshop focused on setting up, maintaining, and safely using a natural indigo vat. We will cover fiber preparation, dipping techniques, and simple resist patterns on cotton and linen.

workshops.title: Natural Dyes Foundations (Online)
workshops.description: A live online introduction to natural dyes for beginners. We will discuss equipment, safety, mordanting, and walk through a basic dye bath together using materials you can gather at home.
workshops.location_address: Online session link provided after registration

workshops.title: Frame Loom Weaving for Beginners
workshops.description: Learn the basics of frame loom weaving in this hands-on in-person workshop. We cover warping the loom, basic stitches, and how to finish a small wall piece.

workshops.title: Weekend Natural Dyes Intensive
workshops.description: A two-day intensive exploring plant-based color on both protein and cellulose fibers. Includes indigo, tannin-rich plants, and modifiers for warm earth tones suited to home decor projects.

workshops.title: Summer Textile Retreat: Weaving, Dye, and Stitch
workshops.description: A three-day in-person retreat combining frame loom weaving, natural dyeing, and hand stitching. Includes shared studio time, group critiques, and gentle prompts for developing a small body of work.

workshops.title: Blue Textiles: Indigo and Iron (Online Lab)
workshops.description: An online lab-style session focused on building depth in blue textiles using indigo vats and iron modifiers. Best suited to students who have basic dye experience.
workshops.location_address: Online session link provided after registration

workshops.title: Designing Textile Wall Hangings for Home Decor
workshops.description: An in-person workshop focused on planning and composing textile wall hangings specifically for home interiors, including scale, color palettes, and installation methods.

workshops.title: Texture and Quilting Lab
workshops.description: A hybrid workshop exploring hand quilting techniques for adding texture and structure to wall hangings. In-person participants work in the studio, while online attendees follow via live stream.

workshops.title: Intro to Weaving on a Simple Frame Loom (Online)
workshops.description: A beginner-friendly online workshop covering loom setup, basic stitches, and finishing a small woven sampler.
workshops.location_address: Online session link provided after registration

workshops.title: Textile Color Lab: Planning Palettes with Natural Dyes
workshops.description: An upcoming online workshop focused on planning cohesive color palettes using natural dyes. Includes pre-session worksheets and live Q&A.
workshops.location_address: Online session link provided after registration

workshops.title: Indigo Shibori Intensive
workshops.description: A two-day in-person intensive exploring stitched, clamped, and bound resist techniques in the indigo vat.
